tickets.title: Data + Memory Architecture — P1-P4 Progressive Alignment
tickets.description: Atlas to review the Data and Memory Architecture document (Phase 4 spec from Ken email 'DATA+MEMORY ARCHITECTURE', sent 2026-05-04). Align the P4 architecture to the P1-P4 progressive build phases defined in the EA roadmap. Output: phased data/memory architecture plan showing what gets built in each phase, dependencies, and gates between phases.
tickets.notes: Assigned to Atlas. Source doc saved to docs/Phase4_DataMemory_Architecture.md (from email 2026-05-04, 20.8KB). Atlas to run after TKT-0103 completes. | Groomed 2026-05-08 14:28 AEST. Add-on lenses confirmed 14:40 AEST: (1) Data classification + use (Client/Internal/System/Operational/Repository/Backup), (2) Data type (relational/structured/unstructured/vector/blob), (3) Additional considerations (encryption, real-time, batch, caching, retention policy), (4) Physical infrastructure architecture (storage type, location, performance tier). Atlas to cover all 4 lenses per phase across P1-P4. Firing Atlas now. | Ken decisions 2026-05-08 15:00 AEST: (1) Anthropic DPA deferred — P1 acceptable, no client data. Gate: must confirm before P2 live. (2) Embedding: nomic-embed-text 768-dim CONFIRMED. (3) P3 REDEFINED — not licensed product. New phase definitions: P2=SaaS individual agents, P3=SaaS company multi-agents (shared context + data), P4=Enterprise/FSI unchanged. DataMemory_P1P4_Roadmap.md requires amendment — P3 section must be rewritten. Flag to Atlas. | All decisions resolved 2026-05-08 15:19 AEST. Final: (1) Anthropic DPA deferred to P2 gate, (2) nomic-embed-text 768-dim confirmed, (3) P3=commercial tier label, (4) P2 isolation=RLS from day one, (5) P3 trigger=formal ROI checklist required — Ken skeptical of P3 value given maintenance cost. Strategic note: P4 enterprise may prefer physical/in-house deployment over P3 SaaS multi-agent — P3 may be skipped entirely in practice. TKT-0046 queued as next Atlas task.

tickets.title: Model3-Policy: Define SOPs and domain boundaries for all Tier 3 agents
tickets.description: AC for TKT-0103. Define formal SOPs and domain boundaries (Model3-Policy) for all Tier 3 (Yoda-Manage-Passthrough) agents: Spark, Ahsoka, Atlas, Thrawn, Lando, Mon Mothma, Krennic. Document: scope limits, what requires Ken/Angie approval, reporting cadence, escalation path, failure handling. Output: Model3-Policy.md in docs/. Ken to confirm before policy is live.
tickets.notes: Design consideration added 2026-05-08 14:49 AEST (Ken): Should Atlas have governance/assurance accountability over all architecture sub-agents and related architectural work? If yes, Atlas moves beyond Tier 3 passive passthrough — gains an assurance role, reviewing outputs of Thrawn, Lando, Mon Mothma, and any future architecture agents to ensure alignment. Implications: (1) Atlas tier classification may need revisiting — hybrid Tier 2/3? (2) What triggers Atlas review — all outputs or selective? (3) How does this interact with Yoda's coordination role? (4) Does Atlas assure just architecture quality, or also governance compliance? DECIDE AS PART OF TKT-0105 GROOMING. Do not implement before Ken confirmation. | DESIGN DECISIONS CONFIRMED 2026-05-10 (Ken): (1) Atlas Option B — Architecture Assurance role. Atlas reviews Thrawn/Lando/Mon Mothma outputs where architectural implications exist. Verdict: ALIGNED/NEEDS-REVISION/FLAG-TO-YODA. Not a blocker, quality gate only. Atlas stays Tier 3. Reviewed to Option C at July QBR. (2) Warden T3 monitoring Option A — Atlas/Thrawn/Lando/Mon Mothma added to model-policy.json for model compliance checks. CHG-0258. Mandate drift monitoring via QBR fleet review (not Warden). Unblocks: write Model3-Policy.md + TKT-0106. | COMPLETE 2026-05-10. docs/Model3-Policy.md written. All 7 T3 agents: Spark/Atlas/Thrawn/Lando/Mon Mothma/Krennic/Luthen. Routing decision tree, domain scope, boundaries, invocation triggers, approval matrix, escalation path, failure handling. Atlas assurance protocol (Option B). Warden T3 monitoring live (19 checks). Unblocks TKT-0106.

tickets.title: Model3-Policy: Apply to existing Tier 3 agents, review and confirm with Ken
tickets.description: AC for TKT-0103. Once Model3-Policy.md is approved (TKT-0105), apply the SOPs and domain boundaries to each existing Tier 3 agent (Spark, Ahsoka, Atlas, Thrawn, Lando, Mon Mothma). Update each agent's SOUL.md/RULES.md to reflect their formal domain boundary and escalation path. Review each agent with Ken before marking as operationalised. Gate: TKT-0105 must be complete and approved first.
tickets.notes: COMPLETE 2026-05-10. Model3-Policy.md block added to all 5 active T3 agent SOUL.md files: Spark (4,332), Atlas (2,850), Thrawn (3,152), Lando (2,737), Mon Mothma (2,936). All within size limits. Key additions: policy ref, invocation rules, hard boundaries, Warden compliance note, scope expansion gate. Atlas: architecture assurance role added. Mon Mothma: DORMANT status + activation gate. Lando: active deliverables listed.

tickets.title: Agent Governance — Squad Model (Tier 5+): Ephemeral Dev/Delivery Squads
tickets.description: Forward-looking governance model for when AInchors spins up ephemeral agent squads for development and delivery work. May require a new tier beyond the current 0-4 framework. Questions to resolve: What is a squad? How are they composed (temporary multi-agent teams)? Who owns squad governance — Yoda or Atlas? How does a squad spin up and tear down? What's the approval gate? How does squad output get assurance-checked before merging back to main? TRIGGER: Review and define BEFORE any development squad work begins. Do not activate until development sprint work is imminent.

tickets.title: Document Generation Pipeline — DOCX/XLSX/PPTX/PDF (Ahsoka Q1 blocker)
tickets.description: Build reusable document generation pipeline for Ahsoka. Required to produce consulting proposals, discovery reports, data exports, and presentations. Ahsoka cannot deliver client outputs without this. Libraries: python-docx (DOCX), openpyxl+pandas (XLSX), python-pptx (PPTX), fpdf+reportlab (PDF). Deliverables: install script, generate-doc.sh wrapper, 4 AInchors-branded templates, test output for each format.

tickets.title: Agile PM / Scrum Master / Agile Coach Agent — Design and Build
tickets.description: Dedicated agent to own sprint facilitation, velocity tracking, QBR packs, epic/milestone management, dependency tracking, and Agile coaching for clients and business users. Three modes: (1) Scrum Master — sprint ceremonies, blockers, velocity, retrospectives; (2) Agile PM — epic tracking, capacity planning, QBR prep, dependency management; (3) Agile Coach — working with external clients and business users to run their own delivery framework. Proposed name: Cassian Andor. Governance: Tier 3 (Yoda-Manage-Passthrough). TRIGGER: multiple concurrent client projects OR external users running sprints OR squad model activated (TKT-0107) OR Yoda PM overhead >40%. REVIEW: July 2026 QBR. Do not build before review.

tickets.title: Process Documentation Framework — User Guides for Business Stream and Clients
tickets.description: Lando (BPM agent) to own process documentation for all product capabilities and features shipped. Documents must be ready BEFORE each release, written for business users and clients (non-technical). Current Notion content is technical and only fit for Ken. Need: (1) Process document template/standard (what goes in a user guide, style, format); (2) Per-capability user guides produced ahead of P2 releases; (3) Integration with sprint/release cycle — Definition of Done must include user doc ready; (4) Delivery format TBD (Notion public page, PDF, HTML). Covers: Nexus features, agent capabilities, Citadel portal, Ahsoka consulting outputs. Assign: Lando. Governance: Tier 3 (Yoda-Manage-Passthrough). Review: as part of P2 release planning.

tickets.title: Angie Agile + Nexus Working Guide — Delivered via Aria
tickets.description: Produce a practical guide for Angie on: (1) How to work within the AInchors Agile framework — sprint cycles, how to raise requests, what to expect each week, how her work gets prioritised; (2) How to work with Nexus multi-agent system — what Aria can do, how to instruct agents, what Ahsoka delivers, how to get the most out of the platform; (3) How to plan and execute business work efficiently using the agents — maximising output, knowing when to delegate vs decide, how to track progress. Delivered BY Aria TO Angie (Angie's pace — not pushed). Format: conversational, non-technical, practical. Lando owns process doc structure. Aria delivers and coaches. DEPENDENCY: TKT-0110 (process doc framework) for template/format standard. Part of Angie onboarding Stage 2+ completion.

tickets.title: obs-collector: cron run failures not captured in obs.db
tickets.description: cron-health-check.sh detects cron consecutiveErrors but does not feed results into obs.db. obs-collector.sh has no check against cron-health-state.json. Result: cron job failures are invisible to the standup 24h error count and obs trend dashboard. Kimi RTB cron failed silently (Day 15) — not surfaced in standup. Fix: add check in obs-collector.sh to read cron-health-state.json failures array and log each as obs event type=cron_run_fail level=ERROR source=cron-health.
tickets.notes: 2026-05-10: Elevated to sprint item. Phantom delegation_fail alerts firing repeatedly (3,761 entries, all empty task/agent/error). Fix: exclude empty-field entries from delegation_fail check in obs-collector.

tickets.title: Fallback alert channel — API-independent notification path
tickets.description: INC-20260509-001 prevention gap: Telegram alerting depends on Anthropic API — same API it monitors. When API hits /bin/zsh, alerts go silent. Need secondary alert path (local script → SMS or email via secondary provider e.g. Twilio/Postmark) that fires when primary API is degraded.
tickets.notes: 2026-05-10: Locked into Sprint 2 (Ken confirmed). Post-mortem INC-20260509-001 action item A1+A4. Build API-independent Telegram alert path — direct HTTP to Telegram Bot API, no Anthropic dependency. Must fire on: balance /bin/zsh, API down, gateway down, health_failure consecutive 3+.

tickets.title: AInchors–Aevlith Technologies partnership agreement — confirm with Angie
tickets.description: Ken to confirm partnership/shareholder agreement between Ainchor Solutions Pty Ltd and Aevlith Technologies Pty Ltd with Angie before ASIC registration proceeds. This is the blocker for TKT-0114 through TKT-0117. Target: this week (w/c 2026-05-09).

tickets.title: Register Aevlith Technologies Pty Ltd with ASIC
tickets.description: Formally incorporate Aevlith Technologies Pty Ltd. Blocked on TKT-0113 (AInchors-Aevlith partnership agreement confirmed by Ken + Angie). Target: next week (w/c 2026-05-12). Domains cannot be secured under company name until ASIC registration complete.

tickets.title: Secure aevlith.ai domain
tickets.description: Register aevlith.ai via Namecheap or Ventra IP. Blocked on TKT-0114 (ASIC registration). Target: next week (w/c 2026-05-12).

tickets.title: Secure aevlith.com.au domain
tickets.description: Register aevlith.com.au (auDA eligibility requires Aevlith Technologies Pty Ltd to be registered with ASIC first). Blocked on TKT-0114. Target: next week (w/c 2026-05-12).

tickets.title: Secure aevlith.com domain
tickets.description: Register aevlith.com if available. Can proceed in parallel with ASIC registration. Target: next week (w/c 2026-05-12).

tickets.title: IP Australia trademark filing — Aevlith (Classes 35 + 42)
tickets.description: File Aevlith trademark with IP Australia. Class 35 (business software) + Class 42 (AI platform services). Lex review required before filing. Blocked on TKT-0114 (ASIC registration). Target: P2 milestone (end-Aug 2026).

tickets.title: RustDesk self-hosted server on OC1 — direct P2P via LAN + Tailscale
tickets.description: Install RustDesk hbbs/hbbr server in Docker on OC1. Goal: eliminate public relay hops. LAN direct P2P on 192.168.3.3 when local. Tailscale P2P via 100.91.60.36 when remote. Ports: 21115-21117 TCP, 21116 UDP.

tickets.title: Spark: LinkedIn image generation via Hugging Face (Flux.1-schnell, free)
tickets.description: Add image generation to Spark using Hugging Face Inference API (free tier, ~1000 req/month). Model: black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-schnell. Flow: Spark generates image prompt from post content → calls HF API → saves image locally → uploads to LinkedIn Assets API → attaches to post. Requires: (1) Ken creates HF account + API key → store in Keychain, (2) Spark SOUL/RULES update, (3) LinkedIn image upload code. Dependency: TKT-0121 (local fallback option).
tickets.resolution: CHG-0254: hf-generate-image.sh, linkedin-upload-image.sh created; linkedin-post.sh --image-asset-urn added; SPARK_RULES.md updated. Blocked on HF API key Keychain setup by Ken.

tickets.title: Spark: LinkedIn image generation via ComfyUI + Flux.1-schnell local on OC1 (fallback/upgrade)
tickets.description: Run Flux.1-schnell locally on OC1 (M4 24GB) via ComfyUI for unlimited free image generation. Setup: install ComfyUI + Flux.1-schnell weights (~6GB). Spark calls ComfyUI API locally. No rate limits, best quality, full control. Dependency on TKT-0120 (HF option) — do this only if HF limits become an issue or Ken prefers full local ownership. OC1 M4 24GB handles Flux.1-schnell well on MPS (Metal).

tickets.title: Fix linkedin-post.sh: add delimiter guard + token delete scope
tickets.description: Two fixes: (1) --content-file parser: add guard that fails loudly if no --- delimiters found (L-020). (2) linkedin-auth.sh: add delete scope to token on next re-auth so API delete works (L-021). Guard already partially patched in this session — full test + CHG needed.

tickets.title: MinIO self-hosted object store on OC1 — interim blob/file access layer
tickets.description: Deploy MinIO on OC1 as S3-compatible interim object store. Goal: bridge remote file access gap before P2 AWS S3 deployment. Addresses generated images and blobs not accessible from Windows/external devices via Tailscale. Expanding use beyond Ken to Angie staff in Malaysia makes this urgent. Scope: (1) MinIO in Docker on OC1, (2) bucket structure per Atlas P2 blob design, (3) presigned URL helper script, (4) update hf-generate-image.sh to store to MinIO + return presigned URL, (5) Tailscale Serve path for MinIO API. Upstream: Atlas DataMemory_P1P4_Roadmap.md P2 blob design. Replaces filesystem MEDIA paths with presigned URLs accessible to all Tailscale peers.
tickets.notes: SCOPE EXPANDED 2026-05-10 09:45: Now also covers (1) Business stream file repository — Angie + KL team upload/share business files and data, (2) Agent business memory layer — Aria and business-stream agents use this as persistent business memory + document access. KL team NOT on Tailscale mesh — access model TBD (pending Atlas assessment). This is now interim Business Data + Agent Memory infrastructure platform, not just image serving fix. | SCOPE ADDITION 2026-05-10 10:01: Brand Code (HBR Agentic Marketing Org framework) folds into this business memory layer. TKT-0124 is not just object storage — it IS the business memory layer for the agentic marketing org. MinIO buckets + metadata layer must support Brand Code: brand strategy, product experience, customer insights, business rules — stored as structured documents accessible by Aria and all business-stream agents. TKT-0127 depends on TKT-0124 as its foundation.

tickets.title: Strategy-to-Backlog Pipeline — formalize roadmap decomposition into actionable tickets
tickets.description: Define and document the formal pipeline for converting strategy/roadmap artefacts (Atlas, Thrawn, Lando) into groomed user stories and sprint-ready tickets. Current gap: roadmaps exist but no ceremony converts them to backlog items. Triggered by Atlas DataMemory roadmap having MinIO/blob solution planned but no ticket raised. Scope: (1) Strategy-to-Backlog ceremony definition — QBR-triggered + mid-sprint triage, Yoda coordinates, Atlas/Thrawn/Lando input, Ken approves. (2) Artefact standard — every roadmap doc must produce a backlog seeding list as a deliverable. (3) DoD gate — strategy artefacts not Done until backlog items raised. (4) Agile Framework v1.0 update with new ceremony. (5) Lando documents under TKT-0110. Dependency: TKT-0110.

tickets.title: Fix LinkedIn post: special character encoding breaks post content
tickets.description: LinkedIn posts with special characters (em dash, smart quotes, unicode) break post content or cause API rejection. Observed in RustDesk post and ongoing content. Fix: (1) audit linkedin-post.sh content extraction for character encoding issues, (2) ensure UTF-8 is preserved end-to-end from draft file → python extraction → JSON payload → API call, (3) test with em dash, smart quotes, ellipsis, unicode. Related: TKT-0123 (delimiter guard). Unblock Spark from using any character in drafts without silent truncation or breakage.

tickets.title: Agentic Marketing Org Design — activate business stream marketing agents for Angie + KL team
tickets.description: Design and implement agentic marketing organisation for AInchors business stream. Informed by: 'Redesigning Your Marketing Organization for the Agentic Age' (HBR, May 2026, https://hbr.org/2026/05/redesigning-your-marketing-organization-for-the-agentic-age). Scope: solution design, proposal, and implementation roadmap for activating marketing agents to work with Angie (CEO, Melbourne) and KL team (Malaysia). Two phases: PHASE 1 (P1 MVP, pre-OC2): activate existing agents (Spark + Aria) for business stream marketing ops, define workflows, integrate with MinIO (TKT-0124) for file/asset sharing, establish Angie+KL team access and collaboration model. PHASE 2 (P2, post-OC2): fully agentic marketing org supporting AInchors internally AND ready to deliver as a service to external clients. Deliverables: (1) current-state assessment of AInchors marketing capability, (2) target agentic marketing org design (agent roles, workflows, tools), (3) P1 MVP implementation plan, (4) P2 transition roadmap, (5) integration with MinIO business repo (TKT-0124). Owners: Spark (marketing), Aria (business stream), Atlas (architecture), Lando (process). Ken + Angie to approve. Dependency: TKT-0124 (MinIO — file/asset sharing for KL team).
tickets.notes: HBR article content loaded 2026-05-10. Framework: 4-layer platform (Brand Code foundation, Execution, Orchestration, Interface) + 5 workstreams (Intelligence+Ideation, Content Creation, Research+Testing, Distribution, Performance+Reporting). P1 MVP focus: Brand Code build + Content Creation + Distribution workstreams, Telegram interface, MinIO file layer (TKT-0124). P2: all 5 workstreams + orchestration layer + external client delivery. Key new concept: Brand Code = machine-readable KB for Aria. Ref: docs/HBR_Agentic_Marketing_Org_May2026_Summary.md | 2026-05-10: Role structure locked. Spark (P1+P2 content+distribution+reporting), Aria expanded mandate (P1 orchestration+Brand Code steward - TKT-0128), Luthen new P2 Marketing Intelligence agent (TKT-0129). No additional P1 agents needed. KL team interface via Angie relay in P1, direct Telegram access in P2. Sub-tickets: TKT-0128 (Aria, HIGH, P1), TKT-0129 (Luthen, MEDIUM, P2).

tickets.title: Aria: expanded marketing orchestration mandate + Brand Code stewardship (P1)
tickets.description: Expand Aria's mandate to cover: (1) Brand Code stewardship — seed and maintain structured brand KB in MinIO (TKT-0124) across brand-strategy, voice-and-tone, ICP profiles, market rules (AU/MY), product catalogue, content rules. (2) Marketing orchestration — receive content requests from Angie/KL team, brief Spark from Brand Code, review Spark output for brand alignment, route to Angie for approval. (3) KL team interface (P1: via Angie relay). Spec: docs/Aria_Marketing_Mandate_Addendum_v1.md. Blocked on: TKT-0124 (MinIO must be live). Sub-ticket of TKT-0127.

tickets.title: Luthen: Marketing Intelligence Agent — design and build (P2)
tickets.description: Design and build Luthen, the Marketing Intelligence Agent. Tier 3 (Yoda-Manage-Passthrough). Owns HBR workstreams 1 (Intelligence + Ideation) and 3 (Research + Testing). Does NOT create content — generates briefs for Spark and feeds learnings into Brand Code. Capabilities: market signal monitoring, competitive intelligence, structured brief generation, A/B test design + analysis, Brand Code contribution. Spec: docs/Luthen_Marketing_Intelligence_Agent_v1.md. Build trigger: OC2 commissioned + P2 client sprint begins + Aria has seeded Brand Code. Sub-ticket of TKT-0127. Blocked on: TKT-0124, TKT-0128.

tickets.title: QBR Agent Fleet Review — mandatory ceremony for mandate, role, scope and TOM optimization
tickets.description: Formalize quarterly Agent Fleet Review as mandatory QBR ceremony. Purpose: ensure agent mandate, role, scope, and governance tier remain fit for purpose as AInchors evolves. Reviews: (1) All active agents vs current TOM — mandate valid? Scope drifted? Utilization right? (2) Governance tier correct? Agents needing tier change? (3) Agent performance — invocation rate, output quality, escalation rate. (4) New agents proposed and approved. (5) Retirements assessed. (6) Mandate conflicts/overlaps resolved. (7) MEMORY.md, Agent_Governance_Framework, SOUL.md updated. Output: Agent Fleet Status Report + TKT/CHG for any changes. Owner: Yoda facilitates, Ken approves. First instance: July 2026 QBR. Cadence: Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct. Integrates with TKT-0125 (Strategy-to-Backlog) and TKT-0109 (Agile PM review). Ken directive 2026-05-10.

tickets.title: Review task log and tracker — completeness, accuracy, gaps
tickets.description: Audit the current task log and tracker system. Review: (1) Is every task being raised as a TKT before work starts (ticket-first rule compliance)? (2) Are tasks being closed with resolution notes? (3) Are sub-tasks and dependencies tracked correctly? (4) Is the Notion AKB Backlog in sync with tickets.json? (5) Any tasks completed without a ticket (shadow work)? (6) Recommend improvements to the tracker if gaps found. Deliverable: gap report + recommendations. Owner: Yoda.

tickets.title: Review cost and ROI log and tracker — accuracy, coverage, insights
tickets.description: Audit the current cost tracking and ROI log system. Review: (1) Is cost-state.json accurate and up to date? (2) Are daily cost snapshots being captured? (3) Is spend being attributed correctly per agent/task? (4) Is there an ROI tracking layer — are we capturing value delivered vs cost incurred? (5) Budget alerts (TKT-0092) working correctly? (6) Recommend: what ROI metrics should we track for P1 → P2 transition (time saved, output volume, error rate reduction)? Deliverable: cost/ROI audit report + recommendations. Owner: Yoda.
